items.headline: Equinix data center permitted near Great Oaks Boulevard
items.summary: A $138 million data center at 131 Great Oaks Boulevard receives permit for construction. Data centers generate local tax revenue and jobs but also consume significant power and water resources, making this a major addition to San José's tech infrastructure footprint.

items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B site work permitted on Rue Ferrari
items.summary: A $35 million site work permit issued for PLD Athena Phase B at 5853 Rue Ferrari. The scale suggests a substantial commercial or mixed-use development in the works, though the permit covers groundwork only.

items.headline: Agreement reached for Seeds of Peace Monument at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The city approved an agreement with The Diane Brandenburg Survivor's Trust for the Seeds of Peace Monument, an artwork by Mario Chiodo, to be located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The monument will be a permanent public art installation at the park.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District 2026-2027 budget approved
items.summary: The council took action on the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District's FY 2026-27 budget report and annual assessment, affecting commercial property owners and businesses in that corridor.

items.headline: City Council updates Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council is scheduled to review an update to the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (covering 2015–2040). This element sets policy direction for emergency preparedness, fire safety, hazardous materials management, and related community health protections that affect land use and development decisions.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the top administrative position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementing council directives.

items.headline: City council to review draft water shortage contingency plan
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will hear an overview of Sunnyvale's Draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city will manage water supply during shortages.

items.headline: Spring grants available for community events and neighborhood projects
items.summary: The City Council will consider applications for the 2026 Community Events and Neighborhood Grant Program's spring cycle on May 18. Residents can submit comments during the public hearing.
